title: Incorrect Error-ENUMVALUE when instantiating a module
body:
Test code:

```verilog
typedef enum logic [1:0] {A, B, C, D } letters;

module my_module
 #(parameter letters LETTER = letters'(0)) 
  ();
endmodule
    
module tb ();
    my_module #(.LETTER(letters'(0))) dut ();
endmodule    

```

I am using (4b3731d31, current master)
```bash
> verilator --version
Verilator 5.003 devel rev v5.002-91-g4b3731d31

> verilator --lint-only --top-module tb bug.sv 
%Error-ENUMVALUE: bug.sv:4:22: Implicit conversion to enum '__024unit::letters' from 'logic[1:0]' (IEEE 1800-2017 6.19.3)
                                : ... In instance tb.dut
                                : ... Suggest use enum's mnemonic, or static cast
    4 |  #(parameter letters LETTER = letters'(0)) 
      |                      ^~~~~~
                  ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/ENUMVALUE?v=5.003
```

Also it would be nice to have if the error showed the location of instantiation instead of definition
